design_plan: Python RNG Execution: seed derived from title='AI agent to build websites', kind='use_case', audience='founders/operators/creators', generated_at='2026-07-06T15:54:00Z' → selected architecture 'workflow-command-center'. AIDA Check: Hero captures Attention with 2–3 line H1; Interest via workflow proof and market context; Desire via computer-use cache advantage; Action via repeated CTAs. Font Selection: Cabinet Grotesk (allowed stack) applied globally. Hero Math Verification: max-width 1120px with clamp() sizing to ensure 2–3 lines on desktop. Bento Density Verification: gapless grids use grid-auto-flow:dense. GSAP Motion Selection: image-scale-fade-scroll + hover-physics guarded for no-GSAP. Label Sweep & Button Check: all primary CTAs black pill linking to Super login; secondary white pill present.
headline: AI agent to build websites — from research to publish, with real computer use

site_brief.audience: founders, operators, and creators who want an AI agent to build and publish websites
site_brief.goal: Create an indexable SEO use-case page in the /use-cases/ cluster. Target the practical query 'AI agent to build websites', show the exact job-to-be-done, and explain how Super can perform the workflow with real computer use and a reusable computer-use cache. Include sibling use-case links so the cluster feels intentional, crawlable, and useful. Add a long-form operator playbook with how-to steps, setup checklist, realistic limits, confirmation moments, and examples that a reader can act on immediately.
site_brief.title: AI agent to build websites
site_brief.use_case_intent: show how Super can research, generate, push to GitHub, publish, and iterate website assets with real computer-use workflows

summary: A practical, operator-grade field guide showing how founders, operators, and creators can use Super as an AI agent to research, build, deploy, and iterate websites using real computer use and a reusable computer-use cache.
